India defeated Pakistan by 61 runs in their ICC T20 World Cup Group A clash in Colombo on Sunday, powered by Ishan
Kishan’s 77 and a collective bowling effort that dismantled the opposition for 114 in 18 overs. India posted 175/7 in 20
overs before bundling out Pakistan, maintaining their unbeaten run with three wins in three matches. With this result,
India remains on top of Group A. Pakistan slipped to third place with two wins and one loss, while the USA hold second
spot with two victories and two defeats. How India Built Match-Winning Total After being asked to bat first, India
suffered an early setback when Abhishek Sharma fell for a four-ball duck, dismissed by Pakistan captain Salman Ali Agha
in the opening over. Ishan Kishan then shifted momentum alongside Tilak Varma. The duo attacked Shaheen Afridi and Abrar
Ahmed during the powerplay, taking India to 52/1 after six overs. Kishan reached his half-century in just 27 deliveries,
registering the third-fastest fifty in India-Pakistan T20I contests. He struck 77 off 40 balls, hitting 10 fours and
three sixes. His innings marked the third-highest individual score in India-Pakistan T20I history, behind Virat Kohli’s
78* and 82*, and Mohammad Rizwan’s 79*. The 87-run partnership between Kishan and Tilak ended when Saim Ayub dismissed
Kishan in the ninth over, leaving India at 88/2. Suryakumar Yadav and Tilak then steadied the innings as India crossed
100 in 11.1 overs and reached 125/2 by the 14th over. Pakistan clawed back in the 15th over when Ayub removed Tilak
Varma (25) and dismissed Hardik Pandya for a golden duck on consecutive deliveries. Suryakumar Yadav contributed 32 off
29 balls before falling in the 19th over. India added late runs, with Shaheen Afridi conceding 16 in the final over as
the total reached 175/7, the highest team score in an India-Pakistan ICC T20 World Cup encounter. Saim Ayub was
Pakistan’s standout bowler with 3/25, while Usman Tariq, Shaheen Afridi and Salman Agha picked up a wicket each.
Pakistan’s Collapse Under Pressure Chasing 176, Pakistan endured a disastrous start. Hardik Pandya dismissed Sahibzada
Farhan for a four-ball duck, and Jasprit Bumrah struck twice in quick succession to remove Saim Ayub and Salman Ali
Agha. Pakistan were reeling at 13/3 inside two overs. Babar Azam struggled for fluency before Axar Patel dismissed him
for 5, reducing Pakistan to 34/4. Usman Khan counter-attacked, scoring 44 off 34 balls with six fours and a six.
Alongside Shadab Khan, he added 39 runs to briefly stabilise the innings as Pakistan reached 71/4 at the halfway mark.

Feb 15 - The United States kept their hopes of reaching the Twenty20 World Cup's Super 8 stage alive as they ended their
Group A campaign with two straight wins, after beating Namibia by 31 runs on Sunday. Captain Monank Patel and Sanjay
Krishnamurthi led with half-centuries as the Americans chose to bat and posted 199-4 in Chennai. Namibia fell well short
as Shadley van Schalkwyk (2-27) and Saurabh Netravalkar (1-27) restricted their flow of runs. Patel, who scored 52 off
30 balls, got the U.S. off to a fast start and combined with Krishnamurthi for an 87-run partnership for the fourth
wicket to get his team to their highest total in the campaign. Krishnamurthi hit six sixes and four boundaries in his
unbeaten knock of 68 from 33 balls, his first half-century in T20 internationals. Namibia started well with Louren
Steenkamp (58) guiding them to 97-1 in 10 overs, but struggled after Steenkamp got caught at point in the 13th over,
ending their innings at 168-6 to lose their third straight game. The U.S., who lost their first two matches to India and
Pakistan, finished with four points, and will hope that their positive net run rate earns them a top-two finish and a
place in the next round. REUTERS

wing. Kempe scored four seconds into a power play midway through the second, putting the puck under the Slovakian

Swedish goaltender's right skate for the equaliser. Pettersson made it 3-2 for Sweden with a five-hole shot late in the

=== line 273 2026-02-15 www.straitstimes.com Cricket-No handshake as Pakistan elect to field against India ===

between the captains before the Twenty20 World Cup Group A blockbuster on Sunday. The match between the bitter rivals
went ahead after Pakistan earlier reversed their decision to boycott the game in solidarity with Bangladesh, who refused

that had eased sanctions on Iran in exchange for curbs on its nuclear programme, and re-applied tough economic sanctions
on Tehran. On Feb 13, a source told Reuters that a US delegation, including envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner,
would meet Iranian officials in Geneva on Feb 17, a meeting later confirmed to Reuters by a senior Iranian official on

MILAN, Feb 14 - The United States came back to beat Denmark 6-3 in the Olympic preliminary stage on Saturday, as the
podium contenders muscled their way to a second win in the men's ice hockey tournament. Matt Boldy scored in the first
period for the Americans while Jack Eichel assisted Brady Tkachuk in the second and netted another goal a minute later.
Defenceman Noah Hanifin, Jake Guentzel and Jack Hughes added goals, restoring order to what had been a surprisingly
tight contest. "It's a 60-minute game, they had a few bounces go their way in the first period," said Eichel. "Give them
credit, they played really hard." The United States play Germany while Denmark play Latvia on Sunday in the final two
Group C games. U.S. goalie Jeremy Swayman lost track of the puck as forward Nick Olesen pushed it over the line in the
second minute, before U.S. forward Boldy grabbed his own rebound and wrapped around the net to level it. Denmark
asserted themselves again midway through the period, when Nicholas B. Jensen fired one in from the blue line in front of
the U.S. bench for a 2-1 lead, and the contest turned testy as Tkachuk got into it with Denmark's alternate captain
Oliver Lauridsen ahead of the intermission. The American winger ripped a wrist shot in midway through the second period
for the equaliser and his linemate Eichel won a faceoff and scored off an assist from Tkachuk's brother Matthew less
than a minute later. Hanifin's rebound tipped off the post and over the line to make it 4-2, but Denmark stayed in it as
defenceman Phillip Bruggisser shot a laser into the back of the net with less than three seconds left in the period. The
U.S. engine was firing on all cylinders in the final period, when the captain Auston Matthews overcame a smothering
Denmark defence to feed the puck to forward Guentzel, who fired it into the far corner for the insurance goal. Jack
Hughes banged one in off the back of the Danish goalie's pad to put the contest out of reach. "I thought the whole group
really stepped up in the third period," said Eichel. "Our team's going to continue to get better as this tournament
goes." REUTERS

PARIS, Feb 15 - French President Emmanuel Macron urged calm and restraint at the weekend after an activist died from
injuries sustained during a beating, an incident that has become a political flashpoint. The 23-year-old died on
Saturday, having fallen into a coma after being violently beaten on Thursday outside a conference given by Rima Hassan,
a far-left member of the European Parliament. Hassan called on Friday for an investigation and for the perpetrators to
be brought to justice, a call that Macron repeated late on Saturday, after a wave of mutual recriminations around the
beating. "It is essential that the perpetrators of this ignominy be prosecuted, brought to justice and convicted. Hatred
that kills has no place among us. I call for calm, restraint and respect," Macron said on X. The activist, whose name
the government has given only as Quentin, had been present to help protect members of the anti-immigration feminist
association Nemesis, which was protesting against the event, the group said in a statement. Le Monde newspaper quoted
prosecutors as saying they had opened an investigation for aggravated manslaughter but that the perpetrators had not
been identified. Politicians quickly weighed in. Bruno Retailleau, head of the conservative Republicans party and a
declared candidate for the 2027 presidential election, blamed the far left. Far-right National Rally (RN) heavyweight
Marine Le Pen said the perpetrators must be brought to justice "with the utmost severity." Jean-Luc Mélenchon, head of
far-left France Unbowed (LFI) of which Hassan is a member, said some of his party's local offices had been "attacked"
following statements by Retailleau and Le Pen. He did not give details. REUTERS

--- articles/2026/02/07/articles.jsonl:1022 T20 World Cup 2026 fixtures, format, teams, groups and how to watch on TV
term Group B
ka, rather than India. They have been replaced by Scotland, the next highest ranked non-qualifier, in the 20-team line-
up. Pakistan are still scheduled to compete, although their government has said that the side will not play against
India in a match that had been due to take place in Colombo on 15 February. The groups are as follows: Group A: India,
Pakistan, United States, Netherlands, Namibia Group B: Australia, Sri Lanka, Ireland, Zimbabwe, Oman Group C: England,
West Indies, Nepal, Italy, Scotland Group D: New Zealand, South Africa, Afghanistan, Canada, UAE The top two from each
group will progress to the Super 8 stage, where each qualifier will play the others in their group in a round-robin
format. No points are carried over from the group stage. Eight venues are being used across two nations. Eden Gardens in
Kolkata, Wankhede Stadium in Mumbai, MA Chidambaram Stadium in Chennai, Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad and Arun
Jaitley Stadium in Delhi will host games in India. There are two Sri Lankan venues in Colombo (R Premadasa Stadium and
Sinhalese Sports Club Ground), with the Pallekele Cricket Stadium near Kandy also utilised. The final will be held in
Ahmedabad, unless Pakistan qualify. In that case, the decider would be held in Colombo. (all times GMT) Saturday 7
February Group A: Pakistan vs Netherlands, Colombo (5.30am) Group C: West Indies vs Scotland, Kolkata (9.30am) Group A:
India vs USA, Mumbai (1.30pm) Sunday 8 February Group D: New Zealand vs Afghanistan, Chennai (5.30am) Group C: England
vs Nepal, Mumbai (9.30am) Group B: Sri Lanka vs Ireland, Colombo

The United States and Iran are engaged in indirect negotiations over Iran's nuclear programme, and different rounds may be held in different countries. criteria:
  • Source of Truth: official announcements from the governments involved in the talks or the mediator, with major wire services such as Reuters or AP as backup
  • Resolution Date: this question resolves when the venue of the Monday round is officially named or the meeting begins, no later than 3rd March 2026
  • Accepted Answer Format: the English name of the host country
--- 489902 In which city will the next technical talks between Iranian and US officials be held in early March 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2026-02-19. Iran and the United States are holding indirect diplomatic talks through mediators over nuclear and regional security issues. A further technical meeting is expected soon after the current round. criteria:
  • Source of Truth: Resolve using the first public confirmation from Iran’s Foreign Ministry, Oman’s Foreign Ministry, or consistent reporting by major international news agencies that identifies the location of the next technical US-Iran talks.
  • Resolution Date: This question resolves no later than 6 March 2026, when the location of that next technical meeting is publicly confirmed.
  • Accepted Answer Format: The answer must be the city name only, in English.
--- 443306 Where will Donald Trump say the final talks before the joint US-Israeli strikes had collapsed by the end of March 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2026-02-19. Diplomatic talks over Iran’s nuclear programme are continuing in international venues. If negotiations fail, later statements may identify the city where the last round broke down. criteria:
  • Source of Truth: A public statement or interview from Donald Trump, supported by transcripts or reporting from major outlets such as Reuters, AP, or The New York Post, by March 31, 2026.
  • Resolution Date: This question resolves when the city of the collapsed final talks is clearly identified in public reporting, no later than March 31, 2026.
  • Accepted Answer Format: Provide the city name only.

Iran would be the “best thing that could happen”, as he sent a second aircraft carrier to the Middle East, ratcheting up
military pressure on the Islamic republic. Go-between Switzerland has played a key role in diplomatic relations between
Iran and the US for decades. Renowned for its neutrality, Switzerland has been representing US interests in Iran since
Washington broke off relations with Tehran after the 1980 hostage crisis, a year after the Iranian revolution. In its
role as the so-called protecting power, Switzerland has for decades allowed the two feuding nations to maintain a

the possibility of a sustained military campaign if the talks do not succeed, US officials have told Reuters. “For the

blocks.content:
Resolution events:
- No new Feb. 10 resolution events were present; the resolved count remained at 150.

Updates made:
- Second Middle East carrier: Feb. 10 Independent/Reuters reporting says Trump is considering sending a second carrier and names USS George Washington and USS George H.W. Bush as the most probable candidates, with USS Gerald R. Ford also possible from the Caribbean. Replaced the stale Carl Vinson/Ford-led forecast with George Washington and George H.W. Bush as the top two.
- Pakistan T20 markets: Al Jazeera reports Pakistan beat the USA by 32 runs for its second win in two games. Reduced the chance Pakistan is the former champion that misses the Super Eight and cut the USA as the clinching-win opponent, while raising Namibia as the likely final clincher.
- Olympic rail sabotage: the Feb. 7 Reuters/Straits Times wording explicitly says the fire targeted the line between Bologna and Venice, and the Feb. 10 anarchist-claim story did not change that geography. Raised Bologna and Venice.
- Tractor FC AFC playoff: Feb. 8 Reuters/Straits Times says Al-Hilal, Al-Ahli, Tractor and Al-Wahda had already booked last-16 places, with Al-Ittihad, Al-Duhail and Sharjah fighting for remaining west spots. Broadened the forecast away from the thin Al Nassr/Al Hilal prior toward the qualified and near-qualified west clubs.
- US-Iran talks venues: Feb. 10 coverage again places active mediation in Oman, with Ali Larijani meeting the Omani sultan and then travelling to Qatar. Raised Oman/Muscat and kept Doha/Qatar as the main alternate.

Watched but no update:
- Bangladesh opposition, Navalny toxin, Deranque city/ambassador, Sudan market strike town, Pakistan motorcycle-bomb district, Ronda Rousey opponent, Israel bobsleigh broadcaster, Benfica racist-remark player, Dutch AI-tool court case, Afghan air-raid provinces, Iranian frigate and Winter Olympics medal/hockey questions had no Feb. 10 signal strong enough for additional changes.

## 2026-02-11 Update

Resolution events:
- Tractor FC playoff opponent resolved to Shabab Al-Ahli. I missed after broadening to several west-region clubs but still omitted Shabab Al-Ahli, even though the Feb. 8 Reuters preview named them among teams vying for the top eight. Lesson: when a preview lists active contenders for a bracket slot, include the named contender even if it is not one of the glamour clubs.

Updates made:
- Bangladesh main opposition: Hindustan Times/Reuters coverage says Bangladesh votes on Feb. 12, with the main contest between BNP- and Jamaat-led coalitions and polls showing a BNP advantage. Raised Jamaat because a BNP win makes Jamaat the likely main opposition, while keeping BNP as the alternate if Jamaat overperforms.
- Second Middle East carrier: Feb. 11 Reuters/Korea Herald repeats that USS George Washington and USS George H.W. Bush are the most likely second-carrier candidates, while Hindustan Times says the Bush has left Norfolk and Ford remains in the Caribbean. Shifted the lead slightly to USS George H.W. Bush, with George Washington close behind.
- Deranque city, Navalny toxin, and Bologna-Venice rail section: raised confidence on the already-strong linked evidence as their resolution dates approach.
- Pakistan motorcycle-bomb district: a Feb. 11 Khaleej Times article places a fresh police attack on the outskirts of Dera Ismail Khan, near Waziristan. Added Dera Ismail Khan as lead for the northwest Pakistan police-station bomb question, while keeping Bannu and North Waziristan tails.
- T20 markets: Pakistan remains top of Group A after beating the USA, while Australia are missing Marsh/Tim David plus the Cummins-Hazlewood-Starc pace core. Raised Australia failure risk, added Ireland as a live alternate for Pakistan's Super Eights opener, and broadened Super Eights Group 1 leader to include Pakistan and South Africa.
- US-Iran talks venues: Larijani travelled from Oman to Qatar, and reporting emphasized Qatar's role as a past mediator. Trimmed Oman/Muscat slightly and raised Qatar/Doha as the main alternate.

Watched but no update:
- Sudan market strike town, Bangladesh cabinet woman, Ronda Rousey opponent, Israel bobsleigh broadcaster, Benfica racist-remark player, Democratic SOTU response, Dutch AI-tool case, Iranian frigate, Lebanon command-centre group, South Africa T20 captain, France-US repost source and Winter Olympics medal/hockey questions had no Feb. 11 signal strong enough for additional changes.

## 2026-02-12 Update

Resolution events:
- Bangladesh main opposition resolved to Jamaat-e-Islami, matching the lead. The BNP-win/Jamaat-opposition read was correct.
- Second Middle East carrier resolved to Gerald R Ford. I kept Ford only as a tail and overweighted George H.W. Bush/George Washington after the Reuters candidate list. Lesson: when an already-deployed carrier is named as a possible option from a nearby theater, keep more mass on the operationally available ship even if press says other candidates are "most likely".
- Deranque city resolved to Lyon, matching the high-confidence linked-event read.

Updates made:
- Dutch AI-tool court case: older UK/French/Indonesian reporting and Feb. 12 xAI coverage keep pointing to Grok as the image tool under explicit/deepfake scrutiny. Raised Grok and trimmed generic image-model alternatives.
- Bangladesh cabinet woman: election-day reporting says the BNP is the frontrunner, Jamaat is its main rival, and Jamaat fielded no women candidates. Added BNP women Rumeen Farhana, Selima Rahman and Shama Obaid while retaining Sharmeen S Murshid as a technocratic/interim-continuity possibility.
- T20 markets: India beat Namibia by 93 runs and now top Group A on net run rate, Sri Lanka crushed Oman for a second win, and England's loss to West Indies leaves them needing two wins. Shifted the former-champion failure risk toward Australia/England and away from Sri Lanka; made Sri Lanka the slight lead for Pakistan's Super Eights opener; added West Indies as a live Super Eights Group 1 leader candidate.
- Pakistan clinching opponent: India and Pakistan both have two wins, but Namibia are 0-2 after losses to the Netherlands and India. Kept Namibia as the lead final-clincher, cut the stale USA mass, and added a modest India tail in case Pakistan clinches by beating India.
- Navalny toxin and Olympic rail: with no contrary Feb. 12 signal and direct prior reporting still intact, raised Novichok and Bologna-Venice slightly before their near-term resolution dates.

Watched but no update:
- Sudan market strike town, northwest Pakistan motorcycle-bomb district, Ronda Rousey opponent, Israel bobsleigh broadcaster, Storm Marta/Leonardo, Benfica racist-remark player, Russia military-police explosion town, Iranian frigate, Democratic SOTU response, France-US repost source, Lebanon command-centre group and South Africa T20 captain had no Feb. 12 naming signal strong enough for additional changes.

## 2026-02-13 Update

Resolution events:
- Navalny toxin resolved to epibatidine. I missed badly by anchoring on the historical Novichok poisoning. Lesson: synthetic-event questions can deliberately use a plausible but non-obvious toxin; leave tail mass for unfamiliar chemicals when wording says "new scientific findings".
- Olympic rail section resolved to Rome and Naples. I missed by over-weighting the first Reuters/Straits Times Bologna-Venice sabotage wording. Lesson: when a question asks about a later investigated cable-burning section, separate the broad disruption story from the exact resolving incident; do not assume the first geography is the final section.

Updates made:
- T20 former-champion failure: Zimbabwe beat Australia by 23 runs and stayed unbeaten, putting Australia in real danger in Group B. Raised Australia sharply, kept England as the second risk after its West Indies loss, and cut Sri Lanka after its 2-0 start.
- Pakistan Super Eights opener: with Sri Lanka and Zimbabwe now the live Group B leaders and Pakistan likely to remain behind India if the India-Pakistan game is rain-affected, added Zimbabwe and lowered Australia.
- Pakistan clinching opponent: Khaleej Times reported a 93% rain chance for the India-Pakistan match in Colombo. A no-result would push Pakistan's likely clinching win back to Namibia, so raised Namibia and cut India/USA.
- Bangladesh cabinet woman: Al Jazeera and Hindustan Times reported BNP's majority and named seven elected women, including Shama Obaid Islam and Rumeen Farhana. Shifted to elected/full-name candidates and removed stale interim/Awami-era names.
- Dutch AI-tool case: Brazil ordered X to block Grok sexualised deepfakes, adding to Indonesia/UK/France pressure on the same product. Raised Grok again.
- Northwest Pakistan police-station bomb: Reuters/Straits Times again tied a fresh police attack to Dera Ismail Khan on the edge of Waziristan. Raised Dera Ismail Khan.
- Winter Olympics 30-medal country: Independent medal-table coverage says the USA trail hosts Italy and Norway. Since 30 total medals would be a new Italian Winter record, raised Italy materially.

Watched but no update:
- Sudan market drone-strike town, Ronda Rousey opponent, Storm Marta/Leonardo, Israel bobsleigh broadcaster, Benfica racist-remark player, Russia military-police explosion town, Iranian frigate, Democratic SOTU response, France-US repost source, Lebanon command-centre group, South Africa T20 captain, Greek migrant-boat island and US-Iran venue questions had no Feb. 13 naming signal strong enough for additional changes.

## 2026-02-14 Update

Resolution events:
- No new Feb. 14 resolution events were present; resolved count remained at 156.

Updates made:
- T20 former-champion failure: England beat Scotland and only need to beat Italy to all but secure the Super Eights, so trimmed England's failure risk. Australia remains the main former champion at risk after the Zimbabwe loss and Ireland's big NRR-boosting win over Oman.
- Pakistan Super Eights opener: Pakistan are still likeliest to finish behind India in Group A, while Sri Lanka and Zimbabwe are the live Group B leaders. Raised Zimbabwe close to Sri Lanka and lowered Australia.
- Pakistan clinching opponent: reporting says the India-Pakistan winner qualifies immediately, while a rain washout would put both teams through on five points. Reduced the overconfident Namibia forecast and raised India as the direct-clinch alternate if Pakistan win the marquee match.
- Super Eights Group 1 leader: South Africa beat New Zealand convincingly for a third straight Group D win, so raised South Africa while keeping India as the lead.
- South Africa T20 captain: Al Jazeera explicitly described Aiden Markram as captain while he led South Africa over New Zealand. Raised Markram materially.
- Men's Olympic ice hockey: Canada and the United States remain unbeaten and strong; Canada beat Switzerland with McDavid producing, the US beat Denmark, Finland rebounded by beating Sweden and Slovakia won Group B. Raised Canada/Finland/Slovakia and trimmed Sweden.
- US-Iran talks: Switzerland/AFP said Oman would host US-Iran talks in Geneva next week, and Reuters said US envoys would meet Iranians in Geneva with Oman mediating. Raised Oman for host country, moved the technical-talks city lead to Geneva, and shifted the final-collapse venue toward Geneva/Muscat split.

Watched but no update:
- Sudan market drone-strike town, northwest Pakistan motorcycle-bomb district, Bangladesh cabinet woman, Ronda Rousey opponent, Storm Marta/Leonardo, Israel bobsleigh broadcaster, Benfica racist-remark player, Russia military-police explosion town, Iranian frigate, Democratic SOTU response, France-US repost source, Lebanon command-centre group, Greek migrant-boat island and Winter Olympics 30-medal country had no Feb. 14 naming signal strong enough for additional changes.
